summary: R2 fixes the QA P1 i18n accessibility finding. Default player-mode shell accessibility strings for virtualized district rows, map-mode buttons, sort buttons, map keyboard help, selected-district live status, map role description, and map renderer failure text now come from packages/ui/src/i18n.ts for en-US, zh-CN, and system fallback. Focused Vitest and Playwright coverage now asserts zh-CN/system fallback accessible names and verifies hard-coded English names do not satisfy the zh-CN path.

tests_run.result: Working tree contains only R2 allowed-path changes in apps/web, packages/ui, tests/e2e, and this handoff.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-APP-SHELL-QUALITY-001

rg -n "Virtualized district rows|Sort by |map mode|Use arrow keys|Selected map district|keyboard navigable map read model|Map renderer failed" packages/ui/src/index.tsx apps/web/src/shell/web-client-shell.tsx
tests_run.result: No matches in implementation code for the QA hard-coded English accessibility string repro set.
Vitest web shell suite passed: 1 test file, 9 tests, including zh-CN player shell accessible-name regression coverage.
Vitest UI suite passed: 1 test file, 15 tests.
Playwright Chromium suite passed: 15 tests, including system zh-CN fallback accessible names and default/player shell flows.
Format, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

decisions.decision: Limited R2 scope to QA's P1 i18n accessibility gap; no release, branding, art, telemetry, server, multiplayer, account, cloud save, manual node battle, protocol, or sim-rule changes were introduced.
Accessible names and screen-reader text in default player mode now use the accepted client i18n catalog instead of JSX string interpolation or hard-coded English.
The shell remains a presentation/read-model coordinator. R2 does not add WorldState ownership, hidden commands, duplicated sim formulas, or authoritative mutations.

risks.summary: Some Playwright assertions still use English labels for the default en-US path. The zh-CN/system fallback path now has explicit negative assertions that those English names do not satisfy localized accessible-name checks.
Raw developer diagnostics remain intentionally available only in explicit debug mode, unchanged from the R1 implementation.

requested_action: Please independently revalidate M7-APP-SHELL-QUALITY-001 R2, focusing on the prior P1 finding: default player-mode accessibility strings should be localized through packages/ui/src/i18n.ts and zh-CN/system fallback tests should fail if hard-coded English labels return.
